articles.title: Suspension lifted for helicopter pilots who hovered near Kid Rock's home
articles.summary: The Army pilots who hovered two helicopters near Kid Rock's Tennessee home during a training run while he clapped and saluted have had their suspension lifted, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Tues

articles.title: Trump plans to attend Wednesday's Supreme Court hearing on birthright citizenship
articles.summary: President Donald Trump plans to sit in on Wednesday's Supreme Court hearing on birthright citizenship, making him the first sitting president to attend oral arguments at the nation's highest court.

articles.title: Food assistance slashed for hundreds of thousands of Rohingya refugees trapped in Bangladesh camps
articles.summary: Hundreds of thousands of Rohingya refugees struggling to survive in Bangladesh's overcrowded camps will see their food assistance slashed starting on Wednesday, raising alarm throughout the increasing

articles.title: Tiger Woods says he'll seek treatment after pleading not guilty to DUI
articles.summary: Woods said Tuesday he is stepping away to seek treatment, four days after his vehicle crashed in Florida and he was arrested on suspicion of driving under the influence. He will miss the Masters for t

articles.title: The Sailors Stranded in the Persian Gulf
articles.summary: Thousands of civilian sailors have been stranded for more than a month in waters surrounded by a conflict zone because of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran.

articles.title: Trump Seeks to Redefine ‘Regime Change’ in Iran War
articles.summary: President Trump and his aides have made contradictory statements on whether the United States and Israel have transformed the Iranian government through violence.

articles.title: Trump Says He Halted Nuclear Threat From Iran, Despite Evidence to the Contrary
articles.summary: For the second time in recent days, President Trump declared that one of the key objectives of the war had been accomplished.

articles.title: Trump Says U.S. Will Be Out of Iran Within Two to Three Weeks
articles.summary: The White House said the president would address the nation about Iran on Wednesday evening.

articles.title: Trump Signs Order Seeking Federal Control of Mail Voting as He Promotes False Claims
articles.summary: Election experts and Democratic officials called the order legally invalid, and Arizona and Oregon pledged to immediately challenge it in court.

articles.title: Justice Dept. Struggles to Respond to Trump’s Suit Against IRS
articles.summary: Officials at the department and the White House are in the middle of a messy and complicated debate over how to respond to President Trump’s lawsuit demanding $10 billion from the I.R.S.

articles.title: Trump signs a new executive order on voting. Experts say he lacks the authority
articles.summary: Trump's executive order seeks to create lists of U.S. citizens who are eligible to vote in each state, and instruct the U.S. Postal Service to send mail ballots only to verified voters.

articles.title: Backdoor Funding of Homeland Security Agency Could Weaken Congress Anew
articles.summary: An emerging Republican plan to skirt a Democratic filibuster and fund an entire department without congressional appropriations would be the latest example of surrendering power to the White House.

articles.title: Who Is Getting Paid During the Department of Homeland Security Shutdown?
articles.summary: At least 120,000 law enforcement officers who work for the agency have continued to collect paychecks throughout the funding lapse. But tens of thousands of workers have gone without pay.

articles.title: Kherson, Ukraine: A hometown changed (Pt. 1)
articles.summary: Four years of Russia's all-out war on Ukraine have transformed not only Ukrainian cities but also how modern warfare is waged, in the first of this two-part story from Kherson.

articles.title: Iranians debate whether the war is worth it
articles.summary: As the war in Iran enters its second month, and President Trump signals an end to the war, many Iranians are urging the U.S and Israel to keep striking their country.

articles.title: Judge rules White House ballroom construction must halt until Congress OK's it
articles.summary: Trump responded to the ruling by complaining that the National Trust for Historic Preservation doesn't appreciate his efforts at "sprucing up" Washington's buildings.

articles.title: Runners misled at a qualifier race will still get invites to the world championship
articles.summary: The U.S. will nearly double its contingent for the women's half marathon championship to fix what officials call an unprecedented problem: an official vehicle took the leading runners off the course.

articles.title: One of the first people known to change their gender was an ancient Egyptian pharaoh
articles.summary: NPR art director and illustrator Jackie Lay tells the story of Hatshepsut, who is widely regarded as one of the greatest pharaohs in Egypt's history — but whose legacy was erased for over 3,000 years.

articles.title: 6 books named finalists for the 2026 International Booker Prize
articles.summary: The shortlisted titles include novels and novellas from authors and translators spanning four continents, with stories that range from Japanese-controlled 1930s Taiwan to the streets of Tehran in 1979

articles.title: DHS resumes asylum decisions. And, Iran's strike injures over 12 U.S. personnel
articles.summary: The Department of Homeland Security has lifted its ban on reviewing asylum applications. And, NPR has confirmed that an Iranian strike injured over a dozen U.S. personnel.

articles.title: Iran war delays delivery of medical goods to other parts of the world
articles.summary: The war in Iran has slowed down international shipping, much of which contains medical and humanitarian goods destined for Asia and Africa.

articles.title: Trump hints at an end to military action in Iran, saying U.S. will leave in 2-3 weeks
articles.summary: Trump's remarks came in response to a question about rising gas prices. He said they would fall once the U.S. leaves Iran, claiming that would happen in "maybe two weeks, maybe three."

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
